info.title: TFC Management API
info.description: The endpoints defined below allow users to programmatically carry out various actions on the tfc platform.

get.description: GET /accounts/2fa/recovery-codes/ - Get remaining count.

post.description: POST /accounts/2fa/recovery-codes/regenerate/ - Generate new codes.

get.description: GET /accounts/2fa/status/ - Current 2FA status.

delete.description: DELETE /accounts/2fa/totp/ - Disable TOTP.

post.description: POST /accounts/2fa/totp/confirm/ - Confirm TOTP with code.
POST /accounts/2fa/totp/setup/ - Begin TOTP setup.
POST /accounts/2fa/verify/passkey/ - Verify passkey as 2FA during login.
POST /accounts/2fa/verify/passkey/options/ - Get WebAuthn options for passkey as 2FA.
POST /accounts/2fa/verify/recovery/ - Verify recovery code during login.
POST /accounts/2fa/verify/totp/ - Verify TOTP during login (Phase 2).

get.summary: Accept an invitation link.
get.description:
GET  — validate the token without consuming it. Returns org info so the
       frontend can render a "Set Password" page.
POST — set the user's password, activate the account, accept the invite,
       and return JWT tokens for auto-login.

post.description:
GET  — validate the token without consuming it. Returns org info so the
       frontend can render a "Set Password" page.
POST — set the user's password, activate the account, accept the invite,
       and return JWT tokens for auto-login.

post.summary: Handle "Launch Software" action from AWS Marketplace
post.description:
This endpoint authenticates an existing AWS Marketplace customer
and redirects appropriately.

This endpoint is called after token verification to create a new user account
for an AWS Marketplace customer.

Verify AWS Marketplace token and redirect to appropriate frontend URL
This endpoint accepts application/x-www-form-urlencoded.

get.summary:
Public (unauthenticated) endpoint returning platform config.
Used by the frontend to decide whether to show region UI.
get.description:
Self-hosted returns cloud=false with no region info.
Cloud returns the current region and available regions list.

post.description: Capture the browser's IANA timezone for the authenticated user.

get.description: Snooze the realtime track for N days (default 7). Daily still fires.
One-click unsubscribe from both digest tracks. Token is HMAC-signed.

get.summary: GET/PUT /accounts/organization/2fa-policy/ - Org 2FA policy.
get.description:
GET is available to all authenticated members (read policy).
PUT is admin-gated inline (Level.ADMIN+) rather than via a permission
class so that a single view can serve both roles without splitting.

put.description:
GET is available to all authenticated members (read policy).

post.description:
Create invites for one or more email addresses.
Also dual-writes to legacy User/membership records for backward compat.

delete.description:
DELETE /accounts/organization/invite/cancel/
Hard deletes the invite record.

post.description:
POST /accounts/organization/invite/resend/
Resets expiration and resends the invite email.

get.summary: GET /accounts/organization/members/
get.description:
Returns UNION of active members + pending/expired invites.
Status is derived at query time (Active / Pending / Expired).

post.description:
Re-activates a deactivated org membership and restores workspace
memberships that were soft-deactivated during removal.  If no prior
workspace memberships exist, the user is added to the default workspace.

delete.description:
Soft-deactivates OrganizationMembership and cascades to workspace
memberships.  Signals handle Redis clear + audit log.

post.description: Update a member's org level and/or workspace level.

get.description: Get all organizations the user has access to.

post.description: Select an organization for the current session.

For users who were removed from their org and want to start fresh.
Only accessible to authenticated users with no current organization.

get.description: Get the currently selected organization for the user.

post.description:
Create a new organization for an already-authenticated user.
Unlike OrganizationCreateAPIView (which is for org-less users),
this allows any user to create additional organizations.
The user becomes Owner of the new org via OrganizationMembership.
Does NOT change user.organization FK (primary org stays the same).

Returns the target org's last-used workspace (from orgWorkspaceMap)
or its default workspace, so the frontend can update both contexts.

patch.description:

Only accessible to Owner or Admin of the organization.

post.summary: Trigger a manual rule run with a sync-or-async branch.
post.description:
Small runs (filter resolves to ≤ ``RULE_RUN_SYNC_THRESHOLD``) finish
in the HTTP request and return 200 with the result — fast feedback
for the common case. Large runs (mostly first-ever runs on backlogs
or rules with wide filters) hand the work to a Temporal activity and
return 202 immediately. The activity emails creator + queue managers
on completion.

The peek is a cheap dry-run (``[:cap+1]`` LIMIT, no COUNT(*)) — sub-
100ms even on 10M+ row trace tables — so this branch costs little
even when it ends up taking the sync path.

post.summary: On-demand error localization for a single dataset cell.
post.description:
Use case: in the dataset detail drawer, the user opens an eval cell
that doesn't have an `error_analysis` block (because the eval was
run before error_localization was enabled, or the user wants a
fresh run). They click "Run error localization" and we:

  1. Look up the cell + its UserEvalMetric (column.source_id) +
     the EvalTemplate.
  2. Resolve the metric's `mapping` (template_var → column UUID)
     against the row's other cells to build `input_data`.
  3. Pull the eval verdict + reason from `cell.value` /
     `cell.value_infos`.
  4. Upsert an `ErrorLocalizerTask(source=DATASET, source_id=cell.id,
     status=PENDING)` so the existing 30s Temporal schedule picks it
     up and processes it via `process_single_error_localization`.

Returns the task id + status. The frontend then polls the cell
detail / task status endpoint until `error_analysis` lands in
`cell.value_infos`.

POST /model-hub/cells/{cell_id}/run-error-localizer/

- prompt_config has new/modified entries → re-run those configs + ALL dependent evals
- user_eval_metrics has new/modified entries → re-run only those evals
- column_id changed → delete old base eval columns, re-run base evals
- If FE sends unchanged data, diffs return empty → no re-run
